title: What To Expect From Upstart's (UPST) Q1 2026 Earnings
text: Before Q1 earnings, the market was expecting Upstart's revenue to grow 43.1% year on year. Looking ahead, sell-side analysts expect revenue to grow 33.4% over the next 12 months, a deceleration versus the last two years. Key Outlook Items: Analysts will be monitoring (1) progress in improving margins for auto and home loan products, (2) the pace at which operating expenses moderate and profitability improves, and (3) milestones in the regulatory review process for Upstart's national bank charter application. Q1 2026 Results: Upstart reported revenue ahead of Wall Street's expectations in Q1 CY2026, with sales up 44.4% year on year to $308.2 million. However, Upstart came out with quarterly earnings of $0.3 per share, missing the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $0.39 per share. More specifically, the company reported Adjusted EPS of $0.31 vs analyst expectations of $0.43 (27% miss) and Adjusted Operating Income of -$7.52 million vs analyst estimates of $12.76 million.
